meta.notes: Directed graph. Nodes = positions. Edges = techniques. Terminal edges = submissions.

positions.name: Standing — Neutral
positions.description: Both players standing, no grips established. Match start position.

positions.name: Standing — Over/Under Clinch
positions.description: One player has an overhook, the other an underhook. Neutral pummeling battle.

positions.name: Clinch — Collar Tie
positions.description: One player controls opponent's head/neck with collar tie grip.

positions.name: Clinch — Body Lock
positions.description: Locked hands around opponent's torso, strong takedown position.

positions.name: Clinch — Double Underhooks
positions.description: Both underhooks secured, dominant clinch position.

positions.name: Clinch — Single Collar Tie w/ Wrist Control
positions.description: Head control with one hand, wrist control with the other. Setup for snaps and shots.

positions.name: Front Headlock
positions.description: Head and arm controlled from front, sprawl-like position. Guillotine and go-behind threats.

positions.name: Clinch — Russian Tie (2-on-1)
positions.description: Two-on-one arm control from standing. Strong off-balancing and takedown setup.

positions.name: Closed Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Bottom player has legs locked around opponent's waist. Classic guard position.

positions.name: Closed Guard (Top — Inside)
positions.description: Top player trapped inside opponent's closed guard. Must defend and work to pass.

positions.name: Open Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Bottom player with feet on hips/legs, controlling distance. General open guard.

positions.name: Half Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Bottom player controlling one of top player's legs between theirs.

positions.name: Half Guard (Top)
positions.description: Top player with one leg trapped in opponent's half guard.

positions.name: Butterfly Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Seated guard with both feet hooked inside opponent's thighs.

positions.name: De La Riva Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: One leg hooking behind opponent's lead leg from outside, sleeve/ankle grip.

positions.name: Reverse De La Riva Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Hook behind opponent's lead leg from inside. Strong for leg lock entries and berimbolo.

positions.name: Single Leg X Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: One leg across opponent's hip, other foot on far hip. Strong sweep and leg lock position.

positions.name: X Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Both legs X'd under opponent with hooks on both thighs. Powerful sweeping position.

positions.name: Rubber Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Closed guard variant with own foot pulled to opposite shoulder. Traps posture.

positions.name: Z Guard / Knee Shield (Bottom)
positions.description: Half guard variant with knee shield frame across opponent's torso.

positions.name: Lasso Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Leg laced over and around opponent's arm from guard. Strong for sweeps and triangles.

positions.name: Spider Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Feet on biceps with grips on sleeves/wrists. Distance management guard.

positions.name: Worm Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Lapel-based guard wrapping opponent's leg. Gi-specific, included for Gi variant.

positions.name: Headquarters (Passing Position)
positions.description: One knee up, one knee down between opponent's legs. Primary passing launch point.

positions.name: Standing Over Open Guard
positions.description: Standing upright with opponent on their back in open guard. Looking to pass.

positions.name: Smash Pass (Mid-Transition)
positions.description: Driving opponent's legs to one side with shoulder pressure. Heavy passing style.

positions.name: Knee Slice (Mid-Transition)
positions.description: Lead knee cutting across opponent's thigh to clear guard. Dynamic passing position.

positions.name: Leg Drag Position
positions.description: Opponent's legs dragged across their body, passer on dominant side angle.

positions.name: Body Fold / Stack Pass
positions.description: Driving opponent's legs over their head, stacking weight forward to pass.

positions.name: Toreando Pass (Mid-Transition)
positions.description: Bullfighter pass — controlling ankles and moving laterally around guard.

positions.name: Long Step Pass (Mid-Transition)
positions.description: Long step around opponent's guard, backstep variation of passing.

positions.name: Side Control (Top)
positions.description: Dominant top position perpendicular to opponent. Chest-to-chest pressure.

positions.name: Side Control (Bottom)
positions.description: Pinned under side control. Must frame and escape.

positions.name: North-South (Top)
positions.description: Head-to-head pin position. Strong for kimuras and chokes.

positions.name: North-South (Bottom)
positions.description: Pinned under north-south. Very difficult escape position.

positions.name: Kesa Gatame / Scarf Hold (Top)
positions.description: Head and arm control with hip-to-hip pressure. Judo-style pin.

positions.name: Kesa Gatame (Bottom)
positions.description: Trapped under scarf hold. Bridge and roll or sneak to back.

positions.name: Knee on Belly (Top)
positions.description: Knee driven into opponent's belly/solar plexus from top. Generates reactions.

positions.name: Knee on Belly (Bottom)
positions.description: Under knee on belly pressure. Must escape or give up worse position.

positions.name: Mount (Top)
positions.description: Sitting on opponent's torso, knees tight. One of the most dominant positions.

positions.name: Mount (Bottom)
positions.description: Pinned under mount. Must escape — extremely vulnerable.

positions.name: High Mount (Top)
positions.description: Mount position with knees in armpits. Arm and neck fully exposed.

positions.name: High Mount (Bottom)
positions.description: Under high mount. Arms trapped, extremely dangerous position.

positions.name: S-Mount (Top)
positions.description: Modified high mount with one leg threaded under opponent's head. Armbar setup.

positions.name: Technical Mount (Top)
positions.description: Modified mount with one hook in, transitioning toward back take.

positions.name: Back Control (Hooks In)
positions.description: Behind opponent with both hooks in. Seatbelt grip. Highest value position.

positions.name: Back Mounted (Defending)
positions.description: Opponent on your back with hooks. Must defend neck and escape.

positions.name: Back Control — Body Triangle
positions.description: Back control with legs locked in figure-four body triangle. Extremely secure.

positions.name: Turtle (Top — Attacking)
positions.description: Behind turtled opponent, working to insert hooks or attack neck.

positions.name: Turtle (Bottom — Defending)
positions.description: Balled up on all fours, protecting neck. Must escape or get taken.

positions.name: Crucifix (Top)
positions.description: Opponent's arms trapped by legs from behind. Devastating control.

positions.name: 50/50 Guard
positions.description: Both players' legs entangled equally. Neutral leg lock position.

positions.name: Standard Ashi Garami (Inside Sankaku)
positions.description: Single leg entanglement with inside position. Straight ankle lock and toe hold territory.

positions.name: Outside Ashi Garami
positions.description: Leg entanglement from outside position. Less control but heel hook available.

positions.name: Inside Sankaku (Honeyhole / Saddle)
positions.description: Triangle configuration on opponent's leg from inside. The premier heel hook position.

positions.name: 411 / Cross Ashi
positions.description: Cross body leg entanglement. Strong control with heel hook and knee bar threats.

positions.name: Game Over Position
positions.description: Double trouble leg entanglement controlling both legs. Maximum leg lock control.

positions.name: Outside Heel Hook Position (Reap)
positions.description: Leg reaped across opponent's centerline with heel exposed. Maximum submission pressure.

positions.name: Scramble
positions.description: Both players mid-transition, no settled position. Athleticism and instinct determine outcome.

positions.name: Deep Half Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Bottom player has underhook deep under opponent with head on far hip. Strong sweep position, opponent partially elevated.

positions.name: Deep Half Guard (Top)
positions.description: Top player partially elevated by deep half guard. Must free leg or counter-attack.

positions.name: Inverted Guard (Bottom)
positions.description: Bottom player inverted, shoulders on mat, hips elevated. Guard recovery and leg lock entry position.

positions.name: Truck Position (Top)
positions.description: Lockdown on opponent's leg from behind with seatbelt. Crossroads between back take, calf slicer, and twister.

positions.name: Truck Position (Bottom)
positions.description: Trapped in truck. Must defend calf slicer, twister, and back take simultaneously.

positions.name: Rear Body Lock (Standing)
positions.description: Locked hands around opponent's torso from behind while standing. Mat return and back take threats.

positions.name: Lockdown (Half Guard Variant)
positions.description: Figure-four leg lock on opponent's trapped leg from half guard. Controls posture and sets up electric chair and sweeps.

techniques.name: Kiss of the Dragon (RDLR Back Take)
Side Control to Knee on Belly
Shrimp / Hip Escape (from Side Control Bottom)
Frame and Re-Guard (from Side Control Bottom)
Roll to Turtle (from Side Control Bottom)
Escape High Mount to Mount Bottom
Free Leg and Pass (Deep Half Top)
Arm Drag to Rear Body Lock
Duck Under to Rear Body Lock
Go Behind to Rear Body Lock
Turn to Face (Rear Body Lock Escape)

counter_techniques.name: Sprawl
counter_techniques.description: Defensive sprawl to stop takedown attempts

counter_techniques.name: Whizzer / Overhook Counter
counter_techniques.description: Overhook to block underhook-based attacks

counter_techniques.name: Posture Up
counter_techniques.description: Break posture control by standing/sitting upright

counter_techniques.name: Stack Defense (Armbar)
counter_techniques.description: Stack weight forward to relieve armbar pressure

counter_techniques.name: Hitchhiker Escape
counter_techniques.description: Turn thumb up and spin body to escape armbar

counter_techniques.name: Von Flue Choke / Pass to Side
counter_techniques.description: Counter guillotine by passing to side control and applying shoulder pressure

counter_techniques.name: Base Out
counter_techniques.description: Post hand or leg to prevent sweep

counter_techniques.name: Post Hand
counter_techniques.description: Post hand behind to prevent sweep

counter_techniques.name: Post Leg
counter_techniques.description: Post leg to prevent scissor-style sweeps

counter_techniques.name: Straighten Arm / Grip Break
counter_techniques.description: Straighten arm to break kimura/americana grip

counter_techniques.name: Strip Grips
counter_techniques.description: Strip opponent's collar/wrist grips to neutralize attack

counter_techniques.name: Frame and Escape
counter_techniques.description: Build frames with forearms and create space to escape

counter_techniques.name: Re-Guard / Recover Guard
counter_techniques.description: Hip escape to re-establish guard position

counter_techniques.name: Re-Guard to Half Guard
counter_techniques.description: Re-establish at least half guard

counter_techniques.name: Hip Escape / Shrimp
counter_techniques.description: Classic shrimp to create space

counter_techniques.name: Elbow-Knee Escape
counter_techniques.description: Work elbow to knee to create guard recovery

counter_techniques.name: Bridge and Roll Defense
counter_techniques.description: Bridge to off-balance opponent from mount/bottom

counter_techniques.name: Underhook Counter
counter_techniques.description: Fight for underhook to prevent pass completion

counter_techniques.name: Crossface Counter
counter_techniques.description: Apply crossface to shut down underhook-based attacks

counter_techniques.name: Backstep Counter
counter_techniques.description: Step back to prevent guard entries and leg entanglements

counter_techniques.name: Strip Hook
counter_techniques.description: Remove DLR or other guard hooks

counter_techniques.name: Pressure Down
counter_techniques.description: Drive weight forward and down to prevent inversions/entries

counter_techniques.name: Hand Fight (RNC Defense)
counter_techniques.description: Two-on-one hand fighting to prevent choke lock

counter_techniques.name: Chin Tuck
counter_techniques.description: Tuck chin to jaw to prevent choke entry under neck

counter_techniques.name: Boot / Heel Defense
counter_techniques.description: Hide heel, straighten leg, or cross feet to prevent heel hook grip

counter_techniques.name: Extract Leg
counter_techniques.description: Pull leg free from entanglement

counter_techniques.name: Straighten Leg Defense
counter_techniques.description: Straighten the knee to prevent kneebar or toe hold

counter_techniques.name: Re-Entangle
counter_techniques.description: Re-enter leg entanglement after opponent attempts escape

counter_techniques.name: Counter Heel Hook
counter_techniques.description: Attack opponent's heel while defending own in 50/50 stalemate

counter_techniques.name: Limp Arm
counter_techniques.description: Pull arm through to prevent D'Arce/Anaconda lock

counter_techniques.name: Roll Through (Omoplata Escape)
counter_techniques.description: Forward roll to escape omoplata position

counter_techniques.name: Stack and Pass (Triangle Defense)
counter_techniques.description: Stack opponent and work to pass around triangle legs

counter_techniques.name: Sit Out
counter_techniques.description: Sit out from turtle or front headlock to recover position

counter_techniques.name: Granby Roll
counter_techniques.description: Shoulder roll to recover guard from turtle or scramble

counter_techniques.name: Spin and Recover
counter_techniques.description: Spin body to face opponent after arm drag attempt

counter_techniques.name: Turn Into Guard
counter_techniques.description: Turn body to face opponent and recover guard position

counter_techniques.name: Create Space
counter_techniques.description: Frame and bridge to create space and prevent choke lock

counter_techniques.name: Grab Own Belt / Lock Hands
counter_techniques.description: Grab own belt or clasp hands to prevent arm isolation

counter_techniques.name: Re-Close Guard
counter_techniques.description: Lock ankles back together to prevent guard opening

counter_techniques.name: Re-Cement Position
counter_techniques.description: Readjust grips and pressure to maintain dominant position

counter_techniques.name: Step Over Defense
counter_techniques.description: Step over entangled leg to prevent leg lock progression

counter_techniques.name: Stuff Guard Pull
counter_techniques.description: Drive forward and smash through guard pull attempt

counter_techniques.name: Invert to Recover
counter_techniques.description: Invert body to re-establish guard during pass attempt

counter_techniques.name: Smash Through Knee Shield
counter_techniques.description: Drive through Z guard knee shield with pressure

counter_techniques.name: Roll Defense
counter_techniques.description: Roll to escape choke or position

counter_techniques.name: Hand Fight
counter_techniques.description: Fight grips to prevent crucifix or choke setup

counter_techniques.name: Push Knee Off
counter_techniques.description: Frame and push knee off belly to relieve pressure

counter_techniques.name: Knee Tap Counter
counter_techniques.description: Counter knee tap to prevent double leg finish

counter_techniques.name: Level Change Re-Snap
counter_techniques.description: Re-snap head down after opponent attempts to level change out

counter_techniques.name: Arm Drag Counter
counter_techniques.description: Counter arm drag by circling and re-facing opponent

counter_techniques.name: Sprawl Bodylock Defense
counter_techniques.description: Sprawl hips to break body lock grip

counter_techniques.name: Uchi Mata Counter Trip
counter_techniques.description: Counter throw by stepping over and tripping

counter_techniques.name: Hip Block
counter_techniques.description: Block hip throw by dropping hips and blocking with own hip

counter_techniques.name: Immediate Pass Attempt
counter_techniques.description: Immediately attack with pass before guard is established

counter_techniques.name: Backstep Imanari Defense
counter_techniques.description: Step back to avoid Imanari roll entry

counter_techniques.name: Pummel Out
counter_techniques.description: Pummel to break body lock grip

counter_techniques.name: Overhook Snap
counter_techniques.description: Use overhook to snap opponent down

counter_techniques.name: Counter Russian Tie Spin
counter_techniques.description: Spin out of Russian tie control

counter_techniques.name: Stack Pass Defense
counter_techniques.description: Stack to counter guillotine pulled to guard

counter_techniques.name: Roll Through Defense
counter_techniques.description: Roll through to escape anaconda choke

counter_techniques.name: Sweep on Stand Up
counter_techniques.description: Attack sweep as opponent stands in guard

counter_techniques.name: Pass Around Defense
counter_techniques.description: Pass around to escape kimura from bottom

counter_techniques.name: Unlock Lockdown
counter_techniques.description: Break lockdown position on trapped leg

counter_techniques.name: Lockdown Defense
counter_techniques.description: Prevent lockdown by keeping leg free

counter_techniques.name: Sit Back Defense
counter_techniques.description: Sit back to prevent Kiss of the Dragon back take

counter_techniques.name: Leg Entangle Counter
counter_techniques.description: Counter leg entanglement attempt during pass

counter_techniques.name: Turn Into Attacker
counter_techniques.description: Turn body into opponent to counter americana grip

counter_techniques.name: Take Back from Turtle
counter_techniques.description: Follow opponent to turtle and take back

counter_techniques.name: Re-Establish KOB
counter_techniques.description: Re-establish knee on belly after escape attempt

counter_techniques.name: Re-Mount
counter_techniques.description: Re-establish mount after escape attempt

counter_techniques.name: Maintain Mount
counter_techniques.description: Adjust base to maintain mount against escape

counter_techniques.name: Re-Take Back
counter_techniques.description: Re-establish back control after escape attempt

counter_techniques.name: Re-Take Turtle
counter_techniques.description: Recapture turtle position after sit out attempt

counter_techniques.name: Follow Roll
counter_techniques.description: Follow Granby roll to maintain top position

counter_techniques.name: Maintain Top
counter_techniques.description: Maintain top control as opponent pulls guard

position_pairs._note: Maps top/bottom positions so the game knows both players' perspectives

blue.description: Intermediate guards, passes, and submission chains

purple.description: Advanced leg locks, inversions, and complex transitions

guard_puller.name: Guard Puller
guard_puller.description: Sweeps, submissions from bottom, guard retention

leg_locker.name: Leg Locker
leg_locker.description: Heel hooks, kneebars, toe holds, leg entanglement transitions

pressure_passer.name: Pressure Passer
pressure_passer.description: Guard passing, smash top game, heavy pressure
